reviews.review_text: Beautiful area, nice comfortable cabins

Website makes it look a lot more attractive than it actually is.  We had booked an ensuite site and when we arrived on Saturday afternoon, the ensuite had not been cleaned after the previous campers. This was rectified after I spoke to reception, and many apologies made. But I do think that an inspection of cabins/ensuites should be made.|There are only a small number of caravan/campervan sites - mostly cabins, some of which seem to be permanently occupied - not that that is a downside. |Very busy over the weekend when we arrived - a long weekend in Victoria, so lots of families, but no noise issues.|Expensive for the facilities provided. See photo of dump point - right next to campers' wash up sink.
Lovely park, but the barking dogs in the neighborhood disrupted the peace unfortunately. Was hoping to find somewhere a little more quiet.

We had the best 2 night stay here, so peaceful! The cabins are great, beds so comfortable, we definitely didn’t want to leave! When we first walked into the cabin, they had left relaxing music on, instantly made us feel at home. The community garden is so pretty! All in all, a fantastic stay and would 100% recommend!

Not many powered sites and weirdly set up for vans - a lot of unusable space at the back of the van and front very close to the road.
Bathroom amenities were very poor, toilet paper not replaced, slippery, small showers and the glass shower door was very hard to open. Very high step into the shower. The amenities is a transportable building, my suggestion would be to replace with a new building. If not, you need to repair and repaint the ceiling. $54 per night for a powered site, perhaps $40 per night would suit the standard of the park.
Quiet neighbourhood, old but fully-facilitated cabins, well-maintained garden. Friendly staff - recommended us good wineries around town. Would recommend.

Grey Nomad. Small quiet park. 4 ensuite sites. 10 powered. Cabins also. Beautiful pool. Very clean. Yes it has permanents but all in beautiful homes. They have fantastic gardens and a reflective garden too. We will cone back

Used to be such a great location with well maintained gardens and facilities. Definitely gone down hill with new owners. Was disappointed to see the state of the property currently and feel for the permanent residents too.
It is situated in a calm, beautiful area, and the location is clean and has all the amenities required, like kitchen appliances. We stayed only one night but recommend it for long stays.
A good caravan park but only 8 sites as the park is mostly cabins.
Visited family, would book into cabins, great location
Was greeted by a nice gentleman got accommodation small cabin was cozy and nice last minute but he looked after us thank you for your service
The environment is excellent, the location is very convenient and close to Healesville town centre, and the staff are friendly and courteous.

Well lets see um ants in bunk bed room kids had to sleep with mum staff fine on arrival useless the rest of the time hair ties in double bed noisy bloody neighbours all night long rooms dirty ask for tea coffee sugar get enough for one person in a family unit the staff expect visitors to clean cabbins as they to lazy i do not recommend a stay here extremely pore no locks on bathroom door oh but there is a lock on the toilet door no covers on doona just a sheet either side of it the room had bad oder found a full rubbish bin never been cleaned

We stayed one night at the cabin. The price was reasonable and the room was clean and tidy, but overall, it fell below expectations. A few small improvements could make a big difference in the experience.
For example, the internet was extremely slow and kept disconnecting, with only one bar of Wi-Fi signal. The towels felt cheap (Temu quality!), and there was no shampoo, shower gel or soap provided in the bathroom. The dishwashing liquid was a strage brand  with Chinese writing on it  (looks bought from Chinese dolla shop) and looked like it had been diluted, surely proper dishwashing liquid doesn’t cost that much.
Overall, I was somewhat satisfied with the stay, but there’s definitely room for improvement with little spending. A little effort could really enhance the guest experience.

It’s in a good and somewhat quiet spot, it’s mostly a retirement village so what campsites they do have are spacious enough. The cleanliness of the bathrooms is non existent so BYO toilet paper and hand soap, most other facilities are quite old and neglected. Couldn’t find a working bbq and there isn’t a camp kitchen unless you like doing your dishes next to the dump point. Funnily the sign out the front says no vacancies even though the sites and cabins are empty 🤷🏽‍♀️

There was a mouse in my cabin. The sheets had hair and crumbs. Couch covers were dirty. Pool was dirty, I had to clean it myself. Mould in shower. Mattress and pillows hard and lumpy. Slight Oder in the cabin. Staff rarely accessible. It was overpriced for what it was.
Good little Park with good facilities. Great for a short getaway.

Very disppointed with this park. At Reception we were ignored for a good 5 minutes while the owner/manager talked to someone behind the desk and then told us to sign a full page of "rules" which seemed to mostly be about eviction and being fined. Quite insulting.  The sites were small and the shower went hot and cold all the time and the amenities weren't clean. For $52 a night, it wasn't good enough.

Stayed on caravan site for a week, nice quiet location.  Owners not on site or available much.  Facilities need upgrading and were not cleaned every day.  'No Vacancy' signs out alot when most cabins & sites were available, strange way to run a park.

If you’re looking for somewhere basic but clean and comfortable, I’d highly recommend Enclave at Healesville (herein after referred to as Enclaves.) Admittedly, I initially only booked it because my first choice of accommodation was booked out; I can say, however, that I’m genuinely happy with my stay.||There are cabins of differing sizes at the Enclaves to suit a variety of different sized groups and/or families. I opted for a basic one bedroom cabin and was very satisfied with the mattress (firm and supportive as I like them) and having a ceiling fan above the bed; I had one of the best nights of sleep with the fan blowing on me. Another thing that I appreciated: good quality blinds, which were fitted to all windows but the bathroom ones. They were beneficial for both privacy and keeping out the light from the outside when trying to sleep.||My only complaints:||1) Showers are on the small side. I’m over six foot tall and a large person so trying to duck under the metal rail to get in the shower and turn around to reach things (eg. Shampoo, soap, etc) was somewhat awkward. If you’re a parent trying to supervise a young child in the shower, it could also be awkward.||2) Split system remote control displays are very difficult to read. It can make for an awkward time if you’re trying to determine if it’s set to cooling or heating. ||A couple of things that I’ll add before wrapping up. NB. This is NOT intended as a criticism of the accommodation OR the staff; rather, more advice for travellers.||1) Be prepared to travel a few kilometres for food or BYO food. Apart from tea and coffee facilities, including milk, there are no options to purchase food on site. I don’t even remember seeing a vending machine. It’s also not like Ballarat or Bendigo where there are an abundance of eateries within walking distance; I had to drive 3-4 km onto the Maroondah Highway to get a meal and buy provisions from the Coles.||2) It’s a gated venue - similar to some workplaces with security passes to staff carparks, visitors and residents (it also has a retirement village part) can only enter if they have a security passes, which is only obtainable with a booking. For this reason, Uber Eats or similar isn’t viable.||The cabins do, however, come with a stove top and a microwave so cooking is an option.
Nice friendly caravan and cabin park.

Limited space for caravans, especially large vans. Facilities ok but not well serviced. Not a lot here but brilliant stop if you want to explore the Healesville region. Staff friendly. Just be mindful that it’s primarily a retirement village or sorts.

**A Hidden Gem of Relaxation and Natural Beauty**

Our recent stay at the Enclave Caravan and Camper Trailer Resort Park Emporium in Healesville was nothing short of exceptional. This charming park combines a relaxed atmosphere with meticulous attention to detail, creating a perfect getaway for those who love the outdoors.

The flora and fauna here are outstanding, with native birds, kangaroos, and possums making appearances throughout the day. The lush greenery, however, truly steals the show. The grounds boast over 850,000 blades of grass across seven specific varieties, including buffalo, couch, Sir Walter, and the incredibly fast-growing Kykuree. Special mention must go to Wen, whose tireless efforts keep the Kykuree in immaculate condition—it’s no small feat!

A curious quirk of the park is the occasional mislabeling of garden signage. Some areas marked as gardens are, in fact, pristine, manicured lawns. This is a minor detail, though, as the quality of these lawns is undeniably excellent and only adds to the park's charm.

Another standout feature is the management’s dedication to personalized service. Repeat visitors are often placed on the same site as before, ensuring a comforting sense of familiarity. It’s thoughtful touches like these that set the Enclave apart from other caravan parks.

The communal BBQ area is a delight, offering a great spot to cook while enjoying the company of fellow travelers. Keep an eye out for the park’s cheeky parrots, who are more than happy to try their luck at nabbing a tasty morsel if you’re not vigilant!

Our distant acquaintances resides in Cabin 16, aptly named "The Echidna." This cabin was beautifully presented and very comfortable. The thoughtful design and cozy furnishings made it a lovely retreat after a day of exploring. The only drawback was the air conditioner, which was noisy to the point of distraction. A little maintenance here would make this cabin truly perfect.  The knives require sharpening too.

All in all, the Enclave Caravan and Camper Trailer Resort Park Emporium is a wonderful place to relax and recharge. Its natural beauty, combined with the care and effort of the management team, creates an environment that is both serene and welcoming. We can’t wait to return—hopefully to the same site! Five stars without hesitation.
Was perfect for us. Very dog friendly.
Great place to stay. Clean rooms, friendly staff. Well maintained.

The staff is fantastic. They are the reason I give this place 5 stars. Otherwise, it is a good value but dated accommodation with free parking and basic essentials. Has a pool and outdoor benches. Location is great but ideally you should drive. No public transport

Stayed for 2 nights. We booked and family cabin with two rooms where photos showed on the web site has one queen and double bunk bed. But we were given a room with single bunk beds for the second bedroom. When inquired, offered no apology and told that how it is. The internet was just so slow too. It is just mediocre place really. Staff was poor in their attitude.
Lovely quiet place.  Cabins were great, staff were wonderful
Lovely pretty caravan park. Very clean facilities. 👌
Very helpful and friendly staff . Nice clean park and cabins are spacious.

We stayed here over the Australia Day weekend and I can say enough about this lovely place. |Kylie and Graem who are the managers here were so helpful in helping us in so many ways |The grounds are perfectly manicured and clean and facilitates are great. ||Will come here again and the best part is it’s a dog friendly park.||Thank you 🙏

Definitely an amazing experience staying here and I love this little cabin!! Much more interesting than normal hotel room. Tho it’s small , I can tell how thoughtful the design it , everything can be fit in the cabin and we got the essentials we need here. There are even heated light and good ventilation in the bathroom , heated blanket in bedroom , microwave , tea , coffee and wine glasses in the kitchen. I’m really surprised by the thoughtfulness way more than the expectation on a 3 star hotel and price.The environment is quiet and relaxing I had good sleep every night. Good location, very close to the town and wineries. Hosts are sweet , we felt so welcomed upon arrival to the day of leaving. Will definitely come again !!
Spotlessly clean, simple cabin in a lovely quiet spot.

A holiday/caravan park that is not interested in taking in holiday makers. There is a percentage of permanents here and the owners were adamant that even if you booked on-line, strict criteria needed to be met or they would turn you away on arrival.

I don’t recommend staying here at the Enclave the owners are racist and are discriminating against people with tattoos.. our family were having a barbecue for a kid’s birthday in there “family friendly” environment (had permission to use the barbecue and pool before even entering from staff) , not long after they came over and told us to pack up and leave immediately while the lady owner looked us up and down seeing that people had tattoos on there body and said others will feel uncomfortable with us being there (that really hit to the heart as tattoos on the body of someone shouldn’t effect how people think of others… pure disgusting behaviour from the owners to even discriminate anyone for how they look🤦‍♀️

The stand alone unit was well appointed, clean and comfortable.  The gounds are well kept, and the staff very friendly and helpful. I highly recommend the Eclave at Healesville, it is very central to many attractions and points of interest.
Small caravan park. Mostly retirement village. Caravan stand ensuites clean and tidy.
This was a lovely holiday park, well run and clean. The owners are lovely and helpful with things to do in the area. We enjoyed our stay.
